pattern: Expanding the three absolute values into their sign choices reduces the problem to maximizing a signed linear combination, which is just the spread of that combination across all indices.
hints: An absolute value is the max of the expression and its negation, so the whole sum of absolutes is the max over independent sign choices for each of the three terms.
For a fixed choice of signs, the value at indices i and j separates into a term in i minus the same term in j, so you want the max-minus-min of that combined quantity.
Enumerate the four sign patterns of (s1, s2) (the i-j sign is absorbed by symmetry); for each compute s1*arr1[k] + s2*arr2[k] + k and take its running max minus min.

edge_inputs.description: mixed signs where the index term matters
extreme opposite magnitudes at the ends
all equal values so only the index distance contributes

stress.description: large arrays with wide value range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    a = [rng.randint(-10**6, 10**6) for _ in range(n)]
    b = [rng.randint(-10**6, 10**6) for _ in range(n)]
    return [a, b]
stress.note: lengths kept equal as the problem requires
